programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: An Andrew Clemens sand bottle circa 1885; a 1933 Goudey baseball card set 239/240; a Joan Miró pencil on paper circa 1965.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.sub-title: The Times That Try Men's Souls
programme.desc: British General Howe defeats General Washington, forcing the Americans to abandon New York; Washington's army regroups in New Jersey before attacking an outpost at Trenton.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: One on One With Steve Adubato
programme.desc: Professionals and innovators share their experiences.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: An expectant father receives a life-threatening diagnosis; Trixie assists a female wrestler after a headlock goes wrong; Dr. Turner learns shocking news that implicates the future of the maternity house.

rating.system: Instituto Nacional de Cine y Artes Audiovisuales
Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Soames commissions a country house despite Irene's distress; as Louisa faces a fresh start, Jo decides his future; Ann reveals a dark secret.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo on Masterpiece
programme.desc: In Paris high society, Edmond meddles in the lives of those he wishes to destroy; Edmond struggles to conceal his identity from Mercedes.

programme.title: Shakespeare: Rise of a Genius
programme.desc: Shakespeare's life and legacy are threatened when the Gunpowder Plot unearths a secret he has worked all his life to bury; when his daughter is implicated in the fallout, Shakespeare produces "Macbeth.".

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação
Instituto Nacional de Cine y Artes Audiovisuales

programme.title: Great Performances
programme.sub-title: Now Hear This: The Call of Istanbul
programme.desc: Exploring the layered sounds of Istanbul where empires met from European classical music to modern jazz.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação
Instituto Nacional de Cine y Artes Audiovisuales

programme.title: Rick Steves' Europe
programme.desc: Three historic capitals of Iran are Persepolis, Shiraz and Esfahan.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: Carl's Concerto; Too Much of a Good Thing
programme.desc: Carl agrees to play the accordion for George's puppet show; Binky tries to keep Buster from eating too many cookies.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.sub-title: Blast From the Past Part I; Blast From the Past Part II
programme.desc: Ozzie and Orli must stop the villainous Knights of the Square Table from turning things to stone; Ozzie and Orli time travel to save Odd Squad.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt Bros. discover there is an old wily orca who only eats large sharks; they find this hard to believe, so with the help of Aviva's dolphin decoder, modified to speak orca, the Wild Kratts go in search of the orca who likes sharks.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: Lily and her family discover a surprise and also embark on a weather hunting expedition.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla and a new friend build a spaceship; Lyla learns her sisters can predict what the other wants, so she tries to do the same with Luke.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: The Sweater Collection; New Bus Stop
programme.desc: Carl loves the new sweater his Pop Pop knit him, but it feels itchy when he wears it; Carl is nervous about his new school bus stop.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel Does Gymnastics; The Big Slide
programme.desc: Daniel is nervous about going to gymnastics; Daniel and O the Owl are excited to ride the big slide.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe learns what privacy signs are and how to use them to communicate to neighbors; Jay gets nervous about getting a haircut.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Rudy is afraid of getting a shot at the doctor's office, so Nina and Abby play pretend to show what the visit will be like so he can feel less scared.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: Survey Says; Lost and Found Patrol
programme.desc: Mr. E has a lot of ping-pong balls and the survey says that he should sell the purple ones; Super's lost her glasses and the Lost and Found Patrol try to find them.

programme.title: Let's Learn
programme.sub-title: What Sounds Do You Hear in Partner?
programme.desc: Exploring mammals and color rhythms; reading "Bahar, the Lucky"; practicing r-controlled vowel ar.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Melting Point; Mr. Raccoon's Bad Day
programme.desc: While delivering chocolates, the kids learn that warmer temperatures can melt treats; the kids help Mr. Raccoon calm down when he is having a bad day.

programme.title: Cyberchase
programme.desc: Hacker positions giant patches of trash in Cyberspace in a plan to finally shut down Motherboard once and for all.

programme.desc: Molly learns how to make offerings, hoping to stop a volcano from erputing.

programme.title: Baking With Julia
programme.desc: Charlotte Akoto prepares meringue cookies, cocoa nests with caramel mousse and a tropical napoleon.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Richard helps a homeowner replace components of his pedestal sink; Heath discusses bathroom vent fans; the team shares home inspection nightmares; Tom explains how to clean dirty tile grout.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Julius Cohen yellow diamond earrings; a 1928 Gibson Granada banjo; a collection of original comic art dating back to 1937.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots With Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
programme.desc: Rebecca Hall and Lee Daniels research their family histories.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: General Howe takes Philadelphia, but the Americans' surprising victory over General Burgoyne at Saratoga encourages France to support the U.S.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: One on One With Steve Adubato
programme.desc: Professionals and innovators share their experiences.

programme.title: Classical Rewind (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: A visual and auditory look at classical masterpieces; host Martin Goldsmith.

programme.title: André Rieu: Power of Love
programme.desc: André Rieu and his Johann Strauss Orchestra perform in Maastricht, Netherlands; music includes Verdi's Triumphal March of Aida, "Dancing on the Stars," a sing-along of "Sweet Caroline" and "The Blue Danube.".

programme.title: Change Your Brain, Change Your Pain With Daniel Amen, MD
programme.desc: Dr. Daniel Amen maps the brain networks that drive physical and emotional pain and reveals his practical, brain-based strategies that help you feel better, think clearer and live more fully.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur's not sure what to make of the new baby; D.W. considers running away to Button Island.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunters find a stowaway in their picnic basket, a little furry dog.

programme.sub-title: The Artist Formerly Known as Liana; Jamaica Day

programme.desc: Carl surprises Sheldon with a lemonade stand, but is confused when Sheldon runs off; Carl's caterpillars turn into butterflies.

programme.desc: Archaeological investigations into the origins of democracy in Greece 2,500 years ago; mass graves and ancient ruins where tyrants were murdered shine light on the birth of a revolutionary form of government.
